key_topics: Number: counting and ordering numbers to 100
Place value: tens and ones in two-digit numbers
Comparing and ordering two-digit numbers
Number sequences: counting in 2s, 3s, 4s, 5s, 10s
Addition and subtraction within 100
Adding 10 and multiples of 10 to any two-digit number
Introduction to multiplication: equal groups, arrays
Multiplication facts for 2 and 10
Division as sharing and grouping
Fractions: halves, thirds, quarters of shapes and numbers
Geometry: describing and sorting 2D and 3D shapes
Symmetry: identifying lines of symmetry
Measurement: measuring length in cm and m, mass in kg and g
Money: recognising coins and notes, adding amounts
Time: reading time to the half hour, using calendars
competences: Understand place value in two-digit numbers and partition them
Add and subtract within 100 using informal and formal methods
Find fractions (half, quarter, third) of shapes and quantities
Measure and compare lengths, masses in standard units
typical_mistakes: Not regrouping (carrying) when adding two-digit numbers
Confusion between sharing and grouping interpretations of division
Thinking larger denominator means larger fraction
Errors reading cm on a ruler (not starting from zero)

key_topics: Reading a variety of texts: fiction, non-fiction, poetry
Comprehension: retrieving information, making inferences
Phonics consolidation: vowel digraphs, split digraphs
Spelling: common patterns, tricky words
Reading fluency: reading aloud with expression and accuracy
Writing: sentences with varied structure
Punctuation: capital letters, full stops, question marks, exclamation marks
Nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs in sentences
Planning and writing short texts: recounts, instructions, stories
Speaking: presenting ideas to the class, role play
competences: Read and comprehend a range of texts appropriate to Stage 2
Write short texts in different forms with correct punctuation
Use phonics to spell unfamiliar words
Speak clearly and listen attentively in discussions
typical_mistakes: Using the same connective throughout (always 'and then')
Not varying sentence openers in writing
Forgetting question marks and exclamation marks

key_topics: Plants: what plants need to grow (water, light, warmth)
Humans and animals: diet and nutrition
Food chains: producer, consumer, predator, prey
Materials: changing state — melting, freezing, dissolving
Solids, liquids, and gases
Movement: pushes, pulls, friction
Light: how we see, opaque/transparent/translucent materials
Sound: vibration, pitch, and loudness
competences: Investigate what plants need to grow and present results
Sort materials by state: solid, liquid, gas
Describe sounds in terms of pitch and loudness
typical_mistakes: Thinking plants get food from soil (not making it through photosynthesis)
